title: Blocking activation stays one-click-after-modal; no extra friction
context: The Memory view's inline vouch offers 'Activate advisory' and 'Activate BLOCKING' in one modal. Concern raised: a mis-click grants real enforcement power. Owner directive: minimum interventions.
decision: Keep the single modal with both buttons. This is already the HC-02 floor (one explicit human act). Safety comes from layers, not friction: advisory is the default-focused button; the CLI refuses --blocking without a P3 proof, so a mis-click can only arm a policy that earned it; audited one-click demotion undoes it; a wrongly-armed proved policy only blocks what its own proof calls a violation. Do NOT add type-the-id confirmation or a second dialog.
consequences: The inline vouch stays one deliberate click — matches the auto-trust philosophy (automate everything, keep exactly one human act where authority is granted).
Future UX passes must not add activation friction without superseding this decision.
alternatives_rejected: Type-the-policy-id confirmation for blocking (rejected: friction on the rare human moment is the opposite of the product's minimum-intervention ethos; the P3 gate + instant demote already bound the risk)
Separate blocking flow in the CLI only, panel advisory-only (rejected: splits the vouch surface; the panel IS the flagship human surface)
